question_text: A lamp converts 120 J of electrical energy into 20 J of light energy and 100 J of heat energy. Calculate the efficiency of the lamp.
explanation: Efficiency = (useful energy output ÷ total energy input) × 100. The useful output is the light energy (20 J), so efficiency = (20 ÷ 120) × 100 = 16.7%. This shows traditional light bulbs are very inefficient because most energy becomes wasted heat.
